derivation: Textbook definition of a data dictionary: a collection of descriptions of the data objects in a data model for the benefit of programmers and others who need to refer to them. Stored procedure is executable code; 'catalog' is a near-synonym in DBMS jargon but the stem is verbatim the data-dictionary definition.

Spreading tablespaces (datafiles) over separate physical disks parallelises I/O and minimises I/O contention on one spindle; it does not enlarge tablespaces nor address fragmentation. Option C reads 'connection' which is almost certainly OCR of 'contention', but it is still the only defensible choice.
Oracle docs: a null is the absence of a value; it represents missing, unknown or inapplicable data. Statement is True.
Data hierarchy largest to smallest: file > record > field (> character > bit). Only D lists File, record, fields.

AND outputs 1 only when all inputs are 1, so 1 AND 1 = 1 (option D). DEFECT: options A and C are byte-identical strings ('1 AND 1 is 0'), a duplicated distractor in the source; the key is still unambiguous.
OR outputs 1 if any input is 1: 0 OR 1 = 1. A, B, D each state an inverted truth-table row.
vi is the standard POSIX/UNIX visual editor; 'iv', 'edit', 'editor' are not UNIX editors.
Executed python3: 17 % 5 = 2 (17 = 3*5 + 2; 3 is the quotient distractor).
HF (3-30 MHz) beyond-horizon links rely on ionospheric refraction, i.e. sky wave propagation. Ground/surface waves attenuate fast; space waves are line-of-sight.
OSI layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application. 'Communication' is not among them.
ipconfig prints the host's own IP/mask/gateway configuration. ping tests reachability, route prints the routing table, SNMP is a management protocol.
An IP address is split by the mask into Network ID + Host ID.
Standard project life cycle order: define, plan, get approval to proceed, implement, evaluate. B plans before defining; C evaluates first.
1 TB = 1024 GB. Petabyte (1024 TB) is larger; 1000 MB ~ 0.98 GB, 100 GB = 0.1 TB, 1e6 KB ~ 0.95 GB are all smaller than 1 TB. Only A is greater than a TB.
